Definition: Saragossa porosa genome assembly, chromosome: 17
Description: Saragossa porosa genome assembly, chromosome: 17
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Noctuoidea; Noctuidae; Hadeninae; Saragossa
Assembly: The assembly ilSarPoro1.hap1.1 is based on 74x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). A large number of telomeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences. Individual heterozygous for an inversion in chromosome 29 (from 31,800Kbp to 2,500Kbp). The exact order and orientation of the repetitive region in chromosome chromosome 28 (3,873Kpb - 4,989Kbp) is unknown.
